chunks.text: EE Distinguished Alumni Award Winners - EECS at Berkeley. EE Distinguished Alumni Award Winners. Distinguished Alumni Awards winners are selected each year by the EE and CS Chairs in consultation with the EECS Faculty Awards Committee and with input from the EECS faculty. Winners receive a plaque awarded at a departmental event, typically the annual BEARS conference.
chunks.question: How are EE Distinguished Alumni Award winners selected?
chunks.answer: By EE and CS Chairs with committee and faculty input.

chunks.text: 2025: Srinivas Devadas (Ph.D. 1988, advisor: A. Richard Newton), Webster Professor of EECS, MIT. DJ Seo (Ph.D. 2016, Advisors: Elad Alon and Michel Maharbiz), Co-Founder and President of Neuralink.
chunks.question: Who are the 2025 EE Distinguished Alumni Award winners?

chunks.text: 2024: David Allstot (Ph.D. 1979, advisors: Robert Brodersen and Paul Gray), Distinguished Special Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering, CMU. Richard Murray (M.S 1988/Ph.D. 1990, advisor: S. Shankar Sastry), Everhart Professor of Control & Dynamical Systems and Bioengineering, Caltech. Kishore Seendripu (Ph.D. 1996, advisor: Theodore Van Duzer), co-founder, CEO, President, and Chairman of the Board of Maxlinear.
chunks.question: Who received the EE Distinguished Alumni Award in 2024?
chunks.answer: David Allstot, Richard Murray, and Kishore Seendripu.

chunks.text: 2023: Colin Parris (M.S. 1987/Ph.D. 1994, advisor: Domenico Ferrari), Senior Vice President and Chief Technology Officer, GE Digital. Dawn Tilbury (M.S. 1992/Ph.D. 1994, advisor: Shankar Sastry), Professor, University of Michigan.
chunks.question: Who are the 2023 EE Distinguished Alumni Award recipients?

chunks.text: 2022: Nickhil Jakatdar (Ph.D. 2000, advisor: Costas Spanos), CEO of GenePath Diagnostics. Bruce Hajek (Ph.D. 1979, advisor: Eugene Wong), Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.
chunks.question: Who won the EE Distinguished Alumni Award in 2022?

chunks.text: 2021: Hsing Kung (Ph.D. 1975, advisor: William Oldham), Managing Partner of Acorn Campus Ventures. George Pappas (Ph.D. 1998, advisor: Shankar Sastry), UPS Foundation Professor and Chair of the Department of Electrical and Systems Engineering, University of Pennsylvania.
chunks.question: Who were the 2021 EE Distinguished Alumni Award winners?

chunks.text: 2020: Willy Sansen (Ph.D. 1972, advisor: Robert G. Meyer), Professor at the Catholic University of Leuven, Belgium. Valerie Taylor (M.S. 1986 / Ph.D. 1991, advisor: David Messerschmitt), Director of the MCS Division of Argonne National Laboratory.
chunks.question: Who received the EE Distinguished Alumni Award in 2020?

chunks.text: 2019: Ahmad Bahai (Ph.D. 1993, advisor: Pravin P. Varaiya), Chief Technical Officer, Texas Instruments. Sharad Malik (Ph.D. 1990, advisor: Robert K. Brayton), Chair of Electrical Engineering, Princeton.
chunks.question: Who are the 2019 EE Distinguished Alumni Award recipients?

chunks.text: 2018: Andrea Goldsmith (B.A. 1986/M.S. 1991/Ph.D. 1994, advisor: Pravin Varaiya), Stephen Harris Professor in the School of Engineering at Stanford. Richard Ruby (Ph.D. 1984, advisor: Theodore Van Duzer), Director of Technology at Broadcom.
chunks.question: Who won the EE Distinguished Alumni Award in 2018?

chunks.text: 2017: Anantha Chandrakasan, Head of the EECS Department, MIT. Wayne Dai, Founder, President and CEO of VeriSilicon.

chunks.text: 1999: William G. Howard, Jr., Independent Consulting Engineer. Noel C. MacDonald, UC Santa Barbara, previously Director of the Federal Microsystems Technology Office at the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency.
chunks.question: Who are the 1999 EE Distinguished Alumni Award recipients?
chunks.answer: William G. Howard, Jr. and Noel C. MacDonald.

chunks.text: 1998: Mohammed Ghausi, UC Davis Electrical and Computer Engineering, Dean Emeritus of the UCD College of Engineering. Barry G. Haskell, Apple.
chunks.question: Who won the EE Distinguished Alumni Award in 1998?
chunks.answer: Mohammed Ghausi and Barry G. Haskell.

chunks.text: 1997: David Auston, Executive Director, Chemical Life Cycle Collaborative (CLiCC) and Director, Carbon Neutrality Initiative at the Institute for Energy Efficiency. James Dao, Founder, president, and CEO, Genyous Biomed.

Robert Pepper, Vice President, Global Technology Policy, Cisco; Founder, Level One Communications (acquired by Intel).
